channel.title: Body Image on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Body Image on MinWeb

item.title: The Weight Penalty: Why Appearance Bias Rules Our Lives
item.description:
Have you ever felt like you were playing a rigged game without being given the rulebook?

If you’ve spent any time on dating apps, professional job boards, or just scrolling through TikTok, you’ve probably felt that sinking sensation. It’s the creeping suspicion that your personality, intelligence, and work ethic don’t actually matter as much as one thing: your weight.

The data confirms what many of us feel in our gut. We live in a society that operates under a brutal, invisible “weight penalty.” It dictates who gets a second date, who lands the promotion, and even how kindly a doctor speaks to you when you’re just trying to get a checkup. (Honestly, it’s exhausting.)

item.title: Why You Should Stop Commenting on People’s Bodies
item.description:
The most important thing you can do for the people in your life is remarkably simple: mind your own business.

Stop commenting on bodies. This applies to the ones you think you are complimenting and the ones you find confusing. Even if you think you are being supportive, your words carry weight you cannot see and history you do not know.

_IMG_PLACEHOLDER

The unintended cost of your words

Saying “You look great, have you lost weight?” is not a harmless way to make someone feel good. A 2024 University of Bristol study found that weight teasing at age 13 predicts higher internalized weight stigma and poorer mental health twenty years later.
